zh.lifeSummary: Robert de Buci於1030年出生在法國諾門迪。他於1079年之前與Judith在艾歐洛結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾凱特林。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Buci
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Markus, Kurt.
Swiss German and South German: from an Alemannic pet form of the personal name Burkhard (see Burkhart ).
Italian: patronymic or plural form of   Buro  .

variants.name: Burkhart
meanings: German: from the medieval personal name Burkhard, from ancient Germanic Burghard, composed of the elements burg ‘fort, castle’ + hard ‘hardy, brave, strong’. This form of the surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine) and in Czechia (compare 2 below), but it is very rare there. Compare also Borgardt , Brookhart , Burgert , Burghart , Burkart , and Burkett .
Germanized form of Polish and Czech Burkert , Czech Purkert or Purkart, and Slovenian Purkart: from a German variant and Slavicized forms of the medieval German personal name Burkhard (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bury
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Bury in Lancashire (now part of Greater Manchester), or from some other similarly named place. The placename comes from the dative case, byrig, of Old English burh ‘fortified place’.

Walloon and French: habitational name from any of several places so named, in Wallonia (Belgium) and Oise (France). The placename is from Buriacum, the name of a Gallo-Roman estate, composed of the personal name Burius + the locative suffix -acum. This surname is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

en.lifeSummary: Robert de Buci was born in 1030, in Normandy, France. He married Judith before 1079, in Europe. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died in Kettering, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert de Buciは1030年にフランスノーマンディで生まれた。1079年以前にヨーロッパでJudithと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼はイギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアケッテリングで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert de Buci(은)는 1030년 프랑스 노먼디에서 태어났다. 그는 1079년 이전 우럽에서 Judith(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 케테링에서 사망했다.
